tracks.meaning: a survey of public opinion or voting preferences, typically conducted by asking a sample of people questions
to conduct a survey or vote by asking people their opinions or preferences
the top of a person's head, especially referring to the crown or scalp
to cut off the horns or ears of an animal
a person's head or the number of individual people (used in phrases like 'per capita' or counting heads)
to obtain information about or monitor the opinions, preferences, or status of computer users or systems
an official count of votes in an election or ballot
